dimensions.name: Press Release Traceability
dimensions.pass_criteria: A stakeholder who has read the Press Release can watch a walkthrough of the demo and immediately recognise the product. The core customer, their problem, and the solution are all visible in the app — not just implied.
dimensions.fail_criteria: The demo looks like a generic app that could be anything. The specific customer persona, problem, or benefit from the Press Release is not evident in the UI, copy, or data shown.

dimensions.name: Core User Journey Coverage
dimensions.pass_criteria: The demo implements the primary user journey end-to-end: the user can start at the entry point, complete the core action described in the Press Release, and see the outcome. The journey has a beginning, middle, and end.
dimensions.fail_criteria: The demo shows isolated screens with no navigable flow, or stops before the user reaches the outcome described in the PR. A stakeholder cannot 'experience' the product — they can only see screenshots of it.

dimensions.name: Structural Runnability
dimensions.pass_criteria: All imported components, modules, and files exist in the project. All npm dependencies in package.json are real, published packages. The vite.config.js and package.json scripts are syntactically correct. npm install && npm start should succeed without code errors.
dimensions.fail_criteria: One or more imported files do not exist in the project. A dependency in package.json is misspelled or fabricated. The start script is malformed. There are syntax errors in any file that would prevent the app from loading.

dimensions.name: Mock Data Honesty
dimensions.pass_criteria: All data shown in the demo is clearly mock data — either obviously placeholder (e.g. 'Acme Corp', 'Jane Smith') or labelled as demo data in the UI or in a comment in the server code. The demo does not imply real backend integration it does not have.
dimensions.fail_criteria: The demo presents mock data in a way that implies a working backend, real integrations, or real user data. A non-technical stakeholder could be misled about what is actually built vs. what is being demonstrated.
